matched: Round 1 matched items still hold: DynamicCache-backed continuation, Qwen2 plumbing (4D causal mask + RoPE + position_ids + cache_position), clean_baseline_acc, encoding='utf-8' blanket, spurious recovery-% print removed, FORBIDDEN_PHRASES gate post-write, effect-size-gated comparative sentence (paired bootstrap, EPSILON_DELTA=0.02, 1000 resamples, seed 0, 95% percentile CI), 5 real sanity tests, t_fixed=20 + TXT header literal, methodology column with exact METHODOLOGY_TAG string, corruption arm mirrored to 4 granularity-matched conditions, determinism (torch.use_deterministic_algorithms(True, warn_only=True) + CUBLAS_WORKSPACE_CONFIG=':4096:8' pre-CUDA + all RNGs seeded), donor freed post-compose, composed state_dict SHA-256 stable guard, environment block fully logged, dataset block mirrors Phase A manifest, sanity mode {no_patch, patch_recovery_full, clean_no_patch, corrupt_recovery_full}×5, __init__ re-exports explicit __all__, FORBIDDEN_PHRASES is strict superset of Phase A inline list

A1 fix confirmed — stage1/run_phase_b.py:43-56 all internal imports are fully-qualified 'from stage1.<...> import ...' (load_config/setup_logging, create_run_dir, FORBIDDEN_PHRASES/check_artifacts_for_forbidden, load_mgsm, load_models/compose_model, parse_answer, exact_match, intervention.patcher surface). Mirrors stage1/run_phase_a.py layout exactly. Unblocks spec §10 canonical 'python -m stage1.run_phase_b' invocation.

A1 guard test confirmed — stage1/tests/test_phase_b_patcher.py::test_run_phase_b_module_imports uses importlib.import_module('stage1.run_phase_b') and specifically distinguishes the bare-package ModuleNotFoundError (utils|data|models|inference|analysis|intervention escaping stage1.*) from unrelated sandbox missing deps. The former raises AssertionError with descriptive message; the latter skips. Asserts presence of run_phase_b, main, EPSILON_DELTA, PHASE_A_CROSS_CHECK_TOL, _load_latest_phase_a_summary, _phase_a_outputs_dir on the imported module — any future drift to unqualified imports fails CI.

A2 fix confirmed — new helper _phase_a_outputs_dir() at run_phase_b.py:125-136 resolves via pathlib.Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1] / 'stage1' / 'outputs' / 'phase_a' (parents[1] = repo root for stage1/run_phase_b.py layout). _load_latest_phase_a_summary() at lines 139-154 globs os.path.join(_phase_a_outputs_dir(), 'run_*', 'phase_a_summary.json') — CWD-invariant. Returns (summary, path) tuple so the resolved path is provenance-logged.

A2 no-match branch FAILs acceptance — run_phase_b.py:696-707: when cross_check_passed is None (no Phase A summary resolved), sanity mode tolerates the miss (bootstrap allowance explicitly labelled 'sanity mode, no prior summary'), but non-sanity runs append (<descriptive FAIL string>, False) which propagates via the 'if not all(ok for _, ok in checks): raise RuntimeError' guard at line 718-719. Spec §11.7 acceptance criterion is no longer trivially-PASS-by-absence — it is a hard RuntimeError in a full run.

A2 guards confirmed — stage1/tests/test_phase_b_patcher.py::test_phase_a_loader_no_match_returns_none monkeypatches _phase_a_outputs_dir to an empty tmp path, chdirs elsewhere, asserts loader returns (None, None). test_phase_a_loader_resolves_repo_relative plants a fake run_20991231_000000/phase_a_summary.json, changes CWD, asserts loader still finds it and the path contains the fake run id — directly proves CWD-invariance.

A3 fix confirmed (option b) — stage1/tests/test_phase_b_patcher.py::test_smoke_marker is gated by pytest.mark.skipif(not _has_gpu_and_weights(), reason=...); the skip reason string contains the verbatim manual command 'python -m stage1.run_phase_b --config stage1/configs/stage2_confound.yaml --sanity --seed 42'. _has_gpu_and_weights checks torch.cuda.is_available() AND transformers AutoConfig.from_pretrained('Qwen/Qwen2.5-1.5B-Instruct', local_files_only=True). When both present, the test runs the subprocess end-to-end in the repo root with a 1800s timeout, asserts exit 0 and presence of the four canonical artifacts (phase_b_summary.txt, phase_b_summary.json, restoration_table.csv, corruption_table.csv) in the latest stage1/outputs/phase_b/run_* directory.

A3 sandbox capability probe documented in writer handoff (cuda_available=false, scipy_installed=false, qwen2_5_1_5b_cached=false) with explicit deferral note and manual command for the production operator.

A15 (R2 nit) fix confirmed — trailing module-scope assert at former line 739 removed; replaced with a comment. The FORBIDDEN_PHRASES invariant is now enforced solely by test_forbidden_phrases_gate, which is the correct mechanism. No import-time AssertionError risk under python -O.
Test #1 tolerance tightened per spec §10 from atol=1e-4 to atol=1e-5 (fp32 bound on CPU-initialised 3-layer Qwen2); passes.

findings.claim: Spec §10 test #3 specifies atol=1e-3 (fp16) for final hidden equality and a 95%-of-5-fixture-prompt token-sequence match clause; implementation narrows to atol=1e-4 on fp32 and drops the multi-prompt greedy-match sub-claim.
findings.evidence: test_all_clean_patch_matches_recipient compares final_patched against composed.norm(clean_states[-1]) with atol=1e-4; the 5-fixture-prompt generate-and-compare sub-test is absent. Writer self-declares this deviation (unchanged from round 1) in writer handoff 'test #3 last-layer equality bound'.
findings.suggested_fix: Non-blocking for the engineering contract (identity-patch + bytewise-equal + state-dict-stable cover the core regressions). If the real-env smoke run produces divergence on pretrained weights, revisit per spec §12 R2. Unchanged from round 1.

findings.claim: test_empty_patch_generate_bytewise_equal compares the DummyTokenizer's whitespace-joined decode, not the raw token IDs. Byte-equality claim relies on the dummy decode being bijective.
findings.evidence: patched_new is parsed from result['output_text'].split() rather than captured as token IDs pre-decode. With the supplied DummyTokenizer this is bijective (space-separated integer strings); any future tokenizer substitution would silently break the contract.
findings.suggested_fix: Expose an optional return_ids hook on run_patched_inference_single (or add a sibling test that calls _greedy_continue_with_cache directly). Non-blocking. Unchanged from round 1; writer acknowledged and deferred to avoid round-2 scope creep.

findings.claim: numpy is imported at module top and is used by the paired bootstrap; writer states it is a transitive dep of torch.
findings.evidence: numpy as np at line 39; used in _paired_bootstrap_diff_ci only.
findings.suggested_fix: Add numpy to the project's requirements-explicit list when Phase C lands. Non-blocking. Unchanged from round 1.

findings.claim: forward_with_patches detaches per-layer outputs to CPU eagerly. On GPU this triggers a D2H copy per layer per sample.
findings.evidence: Unconditional all_outputs.append(hidden.detach().to('cpu')). The production path in run_patched_inference_single discards all_outputs; only tests use it.
findings.suggested_fix: Make CPU offload optional (return_outputs_device kwarg, default-off) if profiling shows this dominates wall-clock. Non-blocking. Unchanged from round 1.

can_ship_with_followup: Run test_smoke_marker on a GPU-equipped host with pretrained Qwen/Qwen2.5-1.5B-Instruct weights cached — command embedded in skip reason: `python -m stage1.run_phase_b --config stage1/configs/stage2_confound.yaml --sanity --seed 42` from repo root. Verify exit 0 and presence of the four canonical artifacts. Spec §11 TXT/JSON literal checks (§11.2/§11.3/§11.10/§11.11) can only be validated by a real run.

Confirm test_run_phase_b_module_imports passes (not skips) in any env with scipy installed — canonical guard against future `from utils.config import ...` regressions. If scipy is present and the test still skips, investigate.

Verify production Phase A outputs directory stage1/outputs/phase_a/run_*/phase_a_summary.json is populated before launching a Phase B full run — spec §11.7 now hard-FAILs on absence in non-sanity mode.
On the real run, confirm which transformers causal-mask path is selected (spec §12 R1) and record it under compose_meta or environment block for provenance.
Consider tightening test_empty_patch_generate_bytewise_equal to compare raw token IDs rather than decoded whitespace-joined strings (carried over from round 1).

A1_imports_qualified.notes: All 8 internal imports prefixed with 'stage1.'; mirrors Phase A. Guard test distinguishes the A1-class regression (bare utils|data|models|inference|analysis|intervention) from unrelated sandbox missing-deps via ModuleNotFoundError.name inspection.

A2_phase_a_glob_repo_root.notes: Glob resolved via pathlib.Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1] / stage1 / outputs / phase_a — CWD-invariant. Non-sanity no-match branch explicitly FAILs the acceptance-check list, propagates through the `raise RuntimeError` guard. Sanity mode tolerates absence (bootstrap) with an explicitly-labelled skip. Resolved path is returned from the loader and logged into phase_b_summary.json.phase_a_cross_check.phase_a_summary_path for provenance.

A3_smoke_test.notes: pytest.mark.skipif(not _has_gpu_and_weights(), reason=<manual command>) gate. _has_gpu_and_weights checks torch.cuda.is_available() AND AutoConfig.from_pretrained(..., local_files_only=True). When prerequisites present, runs subprocess end-to-end (1800s timeout), asserts exit 0 and presence of 4 canonical artifacts. Skip reason contains the exact manual command: `python -m stage1.run_phase_b --config stage1/configs/stage2_confound.yaml --sanity --seed 42`. Sandbox capability probe documented in writer handoff (cuda_available=false, scipy missing, Qwen2.5-1.5B-Instruct not cached) — deferral is environmental, not methodological.

checklist_walk.1_seed: satisfied — unchanged from round 1; _apply_determinism wires random, np.random, torch.manual_seed, torch.cuda.manual_seed_all, transformers.set_seed from --seed before any RNG-consuming op.
checklist_walk.2_determinism: satisfied — torch.use_deterministic_algorithms(True, warn_only=True) + CUBLAS_WORKSPACE_CONFIG=':4096:8' set before any CUDA op; warnings captured under environment.determinism_warnings.
checklist_walk.3_device_dtype: satisfied — device derived from next(model.parameters()).device; no implicit .cuda(); dtype handling defers to HF loader + per-call .to(dtype=hidden.dtype) on patches.
checklist_walk.4_loss_masking: N/A — inference-only; no loss, no train/eval mask asymmetry.
checklist_walk.5_tokenizer_model_pinning: satisfied — recipient/donor revisions from config.models.*_revision; tokenizer loaded from recipient; no hardcoded names in Phase B code.
checklist_walk.6_eval_path_purity: N/A — no DataLoader; plain Python for-loop over samples; no shuffle, no augmentation.
checklist_walk.7_data_collator: N/A — single-prompt inference; tokenizer called per sample with padding=False.
checklist_walk.9_checkpoint_io: N/A — no state_dict save/load beyond HF from_pretrained (revision-pinned); composed model in-memory only; SHA-256 hash verified stable across run (hard RuntimeError on drift).
checklist_walk.10_metric_reduction: satisfied with single-language caveat — per-condition accuracy rows; per-sample 'correct' bool retained in JSONL; per-language breakdown trivially N/A (MGSM-zh single language).
checklist_walk.11_train_eval_leakage: N/A — no splits; MGSM-zh test subset identical to Phase A's pinned loader (read-only) per spec §4.
checklist_walk.12_test_coverage: satisfied — round 2 adds 4 new test cases (test_run_phase_b_module_imports, test_phase_a_loader_no_match_returns_none, test_phase_a_loader_resolves_repo_relative, test_smoke_marker); tolerance on test #1 tightened from 1e-4 to 1e-5 per spec §10. Total 11 tests in stage1/tests/test_phase_b_patcher.py: 7 passing in sandbox, 4 legitimately skipped (scipy missing / no CUDA / no cached weights). Every new/modified file has at least one test: patcher.py (tests #1–#4), wording.py (3 gate tests), run_phase_b.py (module-import guard + Phase A loader guards + smoke marker), intervention/__init__.py (indirect via re-exported imports in tests).
